D6 Display L5 Evaluate Reference

The PQ Curve (ST 2084)

unlike gamma, perceptual quantization ties a code value to an absolute number of nits — the same code is the same light on any HDR display

SDR gamma is relative: code value 1.0 means "as bright as this panel gets." PQ (SMPTE ST 2084) is absolute — it maps each code value to a fixed luminance in cd/m² (nits), from 0 up to 10,000, spaced to follow the eye's contrast sensitivity. A pixel encoded at 1,000 nits is meant to be 1,000 nits everywhere.

Drag the probe and read the nits each curve emits. The PQ curve climbs to 10,000 cd/m²; the SDR gamma 2.4 reference tops out at its peak white. Note the log axis — PQ spends most of its codes below 100 nits, where the eye is most sensitive.
